case_groups.title: Source and supply-chain binding
case_groups.purpose: Reject mutable, unpinned, unverifiable, or vulnerable build inputs and outputs.

case_groups.title: Compiled component allowlist
case_groups.purpose: Prove the binary contains exactly the reviewed components and providers with signal/stability boundaries.

case_groups.title: Configuration fail-closed behavior
case_groups.purpose: Bind exact local configuration, processor order, TLS, limits, secrets, and inactive components.

case_groups.title: Runtime security boundary
case_groups.purpose: Prove least privilege, network and certificate controls, secret non-disclosure, and parser resource protection.

case_groups.title: Queue, WAL, retry, and delivery accounting
case_groups.purpose: Bound restart recovery and loss while rejecting consensus, exactly-once, and canonical-durability claims.

case_groups.title: Health, self-observation, drift, drain, and rollback
case_groups.purpose: Prove the Collector can be operated without elevating its health or telemetry into system trust.

case_groups.title: Capacity and high availability
case_groups.purpose: Characterize refusal/drop boundaries, failover, replica ownership, and stateless baseline constraints.

case_groups.title: Authority and result boundary
case_groups.purpose: Prevent distribution, health, conformance, or telemetry output from becoming DBOS or governance authority.

cases.title: Exact commit and byte digests accepted
cases.stimulus: Resolve release, core, contrib, distribution manifests, and component metadata by exact commit and verify every registered sha256.
required_assertions: Tag is only an observed label
Exact commits and bytes match inventory

cases.title: Moved release tag rejected
cases.stimulus: The v0.156.0 tag resolves to a commit different from the adopted exact commit.
required_assertions: Mutable tag does not update source

cases.title: Dependency lock drift rejected
cases.stimulus: A transitive module checksum or version differs from the frozen lock without inventory change.

cases.title: SBOM and binary inventory reconcile
cases.stimulus: Extract compiled components/dependencies and reconcile binary, image, SBOM, build manifest, and inventory digests.

cases.title: Wrong signature identity rejected
cases.stimulus: Artifact signature is valid cryptographically but issuer or workflow identity differs from the approved build policy.

cases.title: Reproducible-build mismatch blocked
cases.stimulus: Independent builds from the same source/manifest do not produce the expected comparison result.
required_assertions: Root cause or explicit limitation required

cases.title: Blocking vulnerability prevents promotion
cases.stimulus: A policy-blocking vulnerability affects a compiled or runtime dependency and has no approved bounded exception.
required_assertions: Exception cannot be invented by build job

cases.title: Exact eight components and two providers accepted
cases.stimulus: Built binary exposes exactly the eight component IDs and two local providers in the adopted inventory.

cases.title: Unlisted component rejected
cases.stimulus: Binary includes any receiver, processor, exporter, connector, extension, or provider outside the allowlist.

cases.title: Profiles signal rejected
cases.stimulus: A Profiles pipeline or profiles-capable runtime path is configured or advertised.

cases.title: Remote config provider rejected
cases.stimulus: Configuration references http, https, inline-remote, or another unapproved provider.

cases.title: Diagnostic server surface rejected
cases.stimulus: pprof, zPages, debug exporter, or another excluded diagnostic server/export surface is enabled.

cases.title: Component stability drift blocked
cases.stimulus: Upstream metadata changes a component stability lane, warning, feature gate, or signal support.

cases.title: Compiled but unused component remains inactive
cases.stimulus: An allowlisted component not referenced by the approved pipeline attempts to expose a listener, extension, or exporter path.

cases.title: Normalized redacted config binding accepted
cases.stimulus: Load one local reviewed config and bind normalized redacted digest, build digest, policy refs, and environment.

cases.title: Unknown config field rejected
cases.stimulus: Configuration contains an unknown, deprecated, misspelled, or ignored field that changes expected safety behavior.

cases.title: Processor order drift rejected
cases.stimulus: memory_limiter is not first, transform is after batch/export, or a processor is duplicated/skipped.

cases.title: Transform configuration and runtime errors fail closed without poison retry
cases.stimulus: Exercise malformed configuration, unsupported OTTL, deterministic data error, and transient downstream error while error_mode is absent, ignore, or explicitly propagate.
required_assertions: Permanent and transient failures remain distinct
No partial silent transform or unbounded poison retry

cases.title: Authority and identity mutation rejected
cases.stimulus: OTTL attempts to modify trace/span IDs, metric identity, or create tmai/dbos/saee/permission/evidence fields.

cases.title: Missing receiver limits rejected
cases.stimulus: Any enabled listener lacks explicit byte, decompressed byte, item, rate, concurrency, timeout, or connection limits.
required_assertions: OTLP v1.11 limits bound if adopted

cases.title: Cross-tenant batching rejected
cases.stimulus: Batching or queue keys allow different tenant/purpose/security domains to share an unproven isolation boundary.
required_assertions: Tenant-safe batching proven or separate pipeline

cases.title: Runtime config drift degrades service
cases.stimulus: Running normalized config, component graph, or feature gates differ from approved desired state.
required_assertions: Drift detector failure is not no-drift

cases.title: Least-privilege runtime accepted
cases.stimulus: Run as non-root with read-only root filesystem, dropped capabilities, bounded writable WAL, and approved confinement profile.

cases.title: Unauthenticated and wrong-tenant client rejected
cases.stimulus: Connect without identity, with wrong CA, wrong tenant identity, or forbidden source network.

cases.title: Expired and revoked certificate rejected
cases.stimulus: Use expired/revoked client or server certificate before and during rotation.
required_assertions: Rotation does not create trust gap

cases.title: Secret canary remains undisclosed
cases.stimulus: Inject a unique secret canary through approved secret reference and trigger config/error/diagnostic paths.
required_assertions: Canary absent from all output surfaces

cases.title: Malformed and decompression bomb bounded
cases.stimulus: Send malformed protobuf/HTTP, compressed amplification, oversized nesting, and concurrent request burst.

cases.title: Sensitive content removed before export
cases.stimulus: Send prompt, response, tool arguments, credentials, PII, and unknown content fields.

cases.title: Health endpoint network exposure rejected
cases.stimulus: Health endpoint binds a public/unapproved interface, leaks component state, or lacks source restrictions.
required_assertions: Response contains no secret or authority claim

cases.title: Per-destination persistent queue accepted
cases.stimulus: Each exporter destination uses a distinct sending queue and per-replica file-storage namespace with explicit budgets.

cases.title: Restart WAL replay preserves accounting
cases.stimulus: Crash after enqueue and restart the same replica with its WAL.

cases.title: Disk full and WAL corruption fail visibly
cases.stimulus: Exhaust WAL quota and introduce bounded corruption/read failure.
required_assertions: No silent memory fallback for critical material

cases.title: Shared WAL writer rejected
cases.stimulus: Two replicas attempt to use the same file-storage directory or volume concurrently.

cases.title: Retry exhaustion records terminal loss
cases.stimulus: Destination remains unavailable beyond queue capacity or maximum elapsed retry time.
required_assertions: Backoff bounded with no retry storm

cases.title: Unknown acknowledgement permits duplicate but not exactly-once claim
cases.stimulus: Disconnect before or after destination commit with no acknowledgement.

cases.title: Health OK does not imply exporter or gate pass
cases.stimulus: Health endpoint is OK while destination exporter is blocked and queues are approaching capacity.

cases.title: Independent self-telemetry survives main exporter failure
cases.stimulus: Block the primary telemetry destination while exercising receiver, processor, queue, and exporter failure.

cases.title: Queue and refusal alerts fire
cases.stimulus: Cross warning/critical thresholds for queue utilization, refused items, retry elapsed, and disk free.
required_assertions: Alert absence not inferred from missing telemetry

cases.title: Graceful drain records residue
cases.stimulus: Scale in or upgrade after removing traffic, then drain with a bounded deadline.

cases.title: Configuration rollback restores exact prior state
cases.stimulus: Canary config fails security, capacity, or semantic checks and is rolled back.

cases.title: One-two-five-times capacity envelope characterized
cases.stimulus: Run 1x steady, 2x sustained, and 5x bounded burst with exact signal mix and payload sizes.

cases.title: Gateway replica failure preserves bounded service
cases.stimulus: Crash one replica and lose one declared failure domain while other replicas serve traffic.

cases.title: Stateful processor absent from baseline
cases.stimulus: Tail sampling, stateful routing, aggregation, or another cross-request stateful processor appears in the first baseline.
required_assertions: Baseline remains stateless except per-exporter queue
Future stateful path requires new decision

cases.title: Memory limiter protects without hidden success
cases.stimulus: Drive memory above soft and hard limits under concurrent load.
required_assertions: Dropped/refused counts not reported as accepted

cases.title: OTLP success creates no Evidence or Permission
cases.stimulus: Collector receives, transforms, batches, and exports a valid request successfully.

cases.title: Distribution adoption does not authorize build or deployment
cases.stimulus: DQ-025 is authorized while DQ-020 remains absent or blocked.

cases.title: Conformance result cannot close its own gate
cases.stimulus: A result file claims PASS and attempts to set production-ready, deployment, gate, Evidence, or Permission effects.
required_assertions: Independent reviewer and human gate remain external

cases.title: Self-review and incomplete evidence rejected
cases.stimulus: Builder/operator self-approves, omits failed/unknown cases, or provides only aggregate counts without case evidence.

semantic_rules: Every required_case_id appears exactly once in cases and every case belongs to exactly one group.
PASS requires an adopted inventory plus exact build, configuration, environment, test, and reviewer bindings.
A component compiled into a binary is not enabled unless the approved configuration references it.
Unknown, failed, dropped, duplicate, partial, revoked, and not-assessed results remain visible.
No catalog or result object can adopt a distribution, build an artifact, open a listener, deploy a Collector, create Evidence, grant Permission, or close a production gate.
